executive_summary: US-to-Central America crypto remittance market shows strong fundamentals: $45B+ corridor volume in 2024, crypto-powered remittance sub-market at $27.87B globally growing at 25.2% CAGR, with identifiable early adopter segments in crypto-savvy Latino immigrants and Central American diaspora communities.
score_reasoning: Score 72/100 — Data Availability: 20/25 (6+ market sizing estimates from institutional sources including Grand View Research, The Business Research Company, Inter-American Dialogue, World Bank, Emergen Research; published 2024-2025; estimates converge within 2x range). Market Scale (SOM): 19/25 (SOM estimated at $135M-$225M based on US-Central America crypto remittance corridor; conservative $135M estimate used; derived from $45B Central America corridor volume × ~3% crypto penetration × service fee revenue capture). Growth Trajectory: 19/25 (25.2% CAGR for crypto-powered remittances 2025-2030 from The Business Research Company; 16.7% CAGR for digital remittances from Grand View Research; both institutional sources). Early Adopter Identifiability: 14/25 (2 segments meeting all 3 criteria with channels totaling 8M+ members combined). Total: 20 + 19 + 19 + 14 = 72.

tam.source: Inter-American Dialogue 2025 and IDB 2024 — Central America remittance corridor volume in 2024

sam.source: Estimated from Central America corridor ($45B) × ~10% digital-first/crypto-addressable segment based on Grand View Research digital remittance market share data (North America 27.77% of $24.48B global digital remittance market)

som.source: Conservative 3% capture of SAM ($4.5B) in first 3 years, benchmarked against crypto remittance adoption rates and early-stage fintech penetration in the corridor

data.growth_source: The Business Research Company — Crypto-Powered Remittances Global Market Report 2026

early_adopters.segment: Crypto-savvy Latino immigrants in US metro areas (Los Angeles, Houston, Miami, Dallas-Fort Worth) who already use cryptocurrency and send remittances to Central America
early_adopters.evidence_of_spending: Brookings 2023: Hispanics represent 24% of US crypto owners; 92% fintech adoption rate (highest among racial groups); already spend on Remitly, Wise, Western Union averaging $300+ per transaction 16x/year; Bitcoin ATMs clustered in Salvadoran, Colombian, Mexican neighborhoods

early_adopters.segment: Central American diaspora workers using digital remittance apps (Remitly, Xoom, Wise power users) seeking lower fees
early_adopters.evidence_of_spending: Inter-American Dialogue: 6 million households in Central America receive remittances; senders average $300/transaction 16x/year ($4,800/year per sender); already paying 6.49% average fees ($312/year in fees alone); Remitly 90K+ reviews, Western Union 107K+ reviews show active digital adoption

reachable_channels.name: Remitly/Xoom/Wise user communities and app store reviewers
CEMLA Forum on Remittances in Latin America
